Landing

Alliance Network

Vates Alliance Network (VAN)

Building Validated, Market-Ready Ecosystem Solutions for Open Virtualization

The virtualization and cloud infrastructure market is evolving quickly.
Customers are no longer evaluating isolated products. They are looking for validated, coherent solutions they can rely on, evolve over time, and remain in control of.

At the same time, industry consolidation and broader global shifts are pushing organizations to reassess their long-term infrastructure strategies. Stability, predictability, and architectural flexibility have become strategic priorities, and interoperability sits at the root of all three.

Customers also want clarity. They want to see the available options, compare architectures transparently, and make deliberate choices. Making validated solutions visible is essential to preserving real freedom of choice.

What is the Vates Alliance Network?

The Vates Alliance Network (VAN) is Vates’ formal alliance program.

Its objective is straightforward:

To build a structured ecosystem of validated, market-ready solutions that integrate Vates technologies with leading partner solutions, and to make those solutions visible, usable, and commercially effective.

VAN is not a logo program.
It is a solution-led ecosystem initiative designed to turn interoperability into concrete, validated architectures that customers can confidently deploy and that sales teams can confidently position.

VAN positions Vates as a credible, enterprise-grade open alternative in virtualization by surrounding XCP-ng and Vates VMS with a visible, validated, and structured ecosystem.

How VAN Works

VAN operates through a structured framework that connects validation, documentation, and go-to-market execution.

1. Solution Briefs (SB)

Solution Briefs are the atomic validated units of VAN.

Each Solution Brief:

  • Defines a validated combination of Vates and partner technologies
  • Specifies versions, scope of validation, and testing context
  • Clarifies support boundaries and responsibility split
  • Documents known limitations and constraints

Solution Briefs create technical trust.
They answer one essential question:

What is validated, under which conditions, and who supports what?

2. Solutions Compatibility List (SCL)

The Solutions Compatibility List (SCL) is the public catalog of all published Solution Briefs.

The SCL:

  • Makes validated combinations discoverable
  • Provides structured compatibility transparency
  • Acts as a customer-facing trust engine
  • Anchors partner visibility within the ecosystem

For customers, it answers:

What works with Vates?

For partners, it provides:

Credibility, referenceability, and ecosystem positioning.

3. Solution Guides (SG)

Solution Guides operate one level above validation.

Built from one or more Solution Briefs, they transform compatibility into:

  • Clear solution narratives
  • Architecture blueprints
  • Defined use cases
  • Business positioning
  • Deployment guidance

Solution Guides answer:

Why this solution exists, which customer problems it addresses, and how validated components come together into a coherent architecture.

They are the go-to-market backbone of VAN and a practical tool for sales, pre-sales, partners, and customers.

The VAN Flywheel

VAN is designed as a self-reinforcing ecosystem loop:

  1. Co-innovation
  2. Technical validation
  3. Structured documentation
  4. Joint visibility
  5. Coordinated customer engagement
  6. Market adoption

Each validated solution strengthens the ecosystem.
Each successful deployment reinforces credibility.
Each visible reference expands opportunity.

This is how ecosystem momentum compounds.

Benefits for Partners

VAN transforms interoperability into tangible business value.

Technical & Engineering

  • Structured validation and SCL publication
  • Access to non-production (NFR) environments
  • Coordinated engineering alignment
  • Roadmap synchronization (for strategic partners)

Solution & Visibility

  • Participation in Solution Briefs and Solution Guides
  • Inclusion in public solution directories
  • Structured ecosystem positioning
  • Joint announcements and content opportunities

Sales & Business Development

  • Coordinated engagement on customer opportunities
  • Joint account mapping (strategic tier)
  • Shared solution positioning assets
  • Demand-generation collaboration (when aligned)

Enablement

  • Two-way technical and sales training
  • Shared positioning narratives
  • Repeatable solution materials for field teams

Together, we do not simply respond to opportunities.
We structure them, validate them, and amplify them.

Benefits for Customers

For customers, VAN delivers:

  • Reduced deployment risk through validated combinations
  • Clarity of options via transparent solution visibility
  • Interoperability by design
  • Documented architectures ready for evaluation and PoC
  • Confidence in support boundaries

Customers can compare, evaluate, and deploy with confidence.

Participation Model

VAN is selective, structured, and scalable.

Partners are evaluated through a formal qualification process based on:

  • Strategic relevance
  • Technical feasibility
  • Ecosystem value
  • Commitment and reciprocity

Two primary tiers exist:

  • Alliance Member
  • Strategic Alliance Partner

Each tier defines:

  • Validation commitments
  • Marketing participation
  • Sales collaboration expectations
  • Governance cadence

VAN is built on reciprocity, structured collaboration, and measurable contribution.

Governance & Trust

VAN operates under clear governance principles:

  • Formal onboarding and business case evaluation
  • Defined validation standards
  • Version-controlled Solution Brief lifecycle
  • Structured publication workflows
  • Periodic business and technical reviews
  • Clear support and escalation paths

Outdated validations are retired.
Revalidation is scheduled.
Quality is non-negotiable.

Trust is engineered, not implied.

Why VAN Matters Now

As infrastructure decisions become strategic, organizations seek:

  • Long-term architectural flexibility
  • Reduced exposure to unilateral ecosystem shifts
  • Transparency in compatibility and support
  • A credible open alternative

VAN exists to meet that expectation.

By structuring the ecosystem around validated solutions, Vates ensures that openness translates into operational confidence and commercial effectiveness.

Join the Ecosystem

VAN is designed for technology vendors, hardware manufacturers, ISVs, system integrators, cloud providers, and service partners who believe that:

  • Interoperability is foundational
  • Transparency builds trust
  • Ecosystems create stronger outcomes than isolated products

If you are building complementary technologies and want to transform interoperability into visible, validated, market-ready solutions:

Let’s structure it together.

Explore VAN Participation

Interested in joining the Vates Alliance Network?

  • Review the VAN Program Overview
  • Discover published Solution Briefs
  • Contact the Alliance Team

Contact us to explore how we can build and amplify solutions together.

Vates Alliance Network (VAN)
